purpose: The commit fixes a bug where commands were queued after HCI_UNREGISTER was set, leading to potential use-after-free issues.
Removes an unnecessary header include to clean up code dependencies.
The commit updates documentation to include a new 'N/A' metric for store operations in perf c2c.
The commit addresses race conditions and lock handling in hugetlb during vma split and unmapping by ensuring proper allocation and freeing of vma locks.
The commit adds documentation for AVDD and MICVDD power supplies in the device tree bindings for Realtek rt5682s, improving code maintainability.
The commit fixes a resource leak by adding an iounmap() call in the remove function to properly release resources that were previously not freed.
Removes an unused 'work' field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in kTLS code.
The commit changes the type of key_idx from unsigned to signed to fix warnings when -1 is assigned.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfig files without any functional changes, purely for organization.
The commit introduces a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace direct condition checks, improving code readability.
The commit modifies self-test cases to skip tests when the Real-Time Clock (RTC) is not present, preventing unnecessary failures.
Removes a redundant variable assignment and addresses a dead code warning, improving code quality.
The commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing rcuscale to function without requiring RCU Tasks. This reduces kernel size and complexity by removing unnecessary dependencies.
The commit corrects a duplicated phrase in a code comment, improving readability without affecting functionality.
The commit reverts changes to how the clock table is populated in AMD display drivers to fix an issue causing underflow when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds documentation for Renesas RZ/V2L SoC in Mali Bifrost GPU device tree bindings.
The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across multiple files in the DW PCIe controller code to maintain consistent naming conventions.
The commit fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier in a debug message to match the argument type, ensuring proper data formatting and preventing potential issues.
The commit fixes a bug in the branch range check for MIPS jump labels by correctly casting BIT(25) to long, preventing incorrect warnings due to integer overflow or sign issues.

The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU() and directly calls rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd(). This is part of an ongoing effort to eliminate an unwanted HAL layer, simplifying the code structure.
The commit changes a non-global symbol to static to fix sparse warnings.
The commit corrects the device tree bindings by removing incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes, ensuring proper interrupt configuration.
The commit refactors array allocation in struct ctrl_ref to improve memory management, preparing for driver-controlled array sizing.
This commit adds support for additional SoCs and PMICs in the socinfo driver by including new entries in the pmic_models and soc_id arrays.

The commit improves the recognition of all possible chip cuts (versions) for Realtek Wi-Fi drivers by expanding from 'A' to 'P'. It simplifies the code by replacing a switch statement with a direct calculation, enhancing logging accuracy.
Removes the custom .is_enabled state from PRCMU clocks, relying instead on the core's software enable count to avoid redundancy.

The commit aims to reduce and organize the usage of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL in the RISC-V memory management initialization code. It moves and groups related preprocessor directives to improve code maintainability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for Qualcomm SM6350, allowing I2C nodes to function.
The commit improves code comments by clarifying descriptions, fixing typos, and adjusting code style for better readability.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when failing to acquire a hardware semaphore during resource release, particularly if hardware is removed unexpectedly.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI terminology by renaming variables and error messages related to master/slave mode to provider/consumer mode.
The commit removes an unnecessary code block related to IS_SWAPFILE, which was always defined as 0. This change cleans up the code without affecting functionality.
The commit adds functionality to handle GTP Echo Requests by sending appropriate responses, ensuring compliance with the GTP specification.
The commit replaces cpumask_next functions with cpumask_first where appropriate for efficiency and adds cpumask_first_zero to find the first unset CPU.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR which was causing console errors during STA roaming due to unsupported firmware calls.
The commit adds support for a new platform configuration without an amplifier by introducing a new board config and updating ACPI match tables.
The commit adds a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg requests when the socket does not support it, preventing potential bugs related to unsupported operations.
The commit modifies the LaTeX style file to conditionally load xeCJK.sty only if it exists, preventing errors when the package isn't installed. It addresses a known issue on RHEL 9.
The commit modifies the checkpatch.pl script to allow 'case' macros without triggering errors, improving code style checking.
The commit refactors the simpledrm driver to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers. This improves code structure and maintainability without introducing new features or fixing bugs.
The commit adds a JPEG decode worker interface to manage parallel execution across multiple hardware units on MT8195.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK debug statements and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb function, as the information is already provided by tracepoints. This cleans up the code and reduces log noise.
The commit adds support for entering a low-power zstate during extended vertical blanking periods in AMD display drivers, optimizing power usage.

The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback function pointer, correcting the parameter type from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type. This addresses a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure observed when using Clang's kernel CFI on certain hardware.
The commit corrects a property name in the device tree binding from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false'. This ensures proper functionality and prevents invalid configurations.
The commit silences an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by modifying the Makefile to disable the warning for a specific object file.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify the code.

The commit reorganizes the io_uring subsystem by moving fixed file installation and removal functions to be with other filetable-related code. It changes function parameters from using an io_kiocb request object to using an io_ring_ctx context object, improving code organization and clarity.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes, allowing independent configuration and control.
The commit simplifies the code by removing an unused macro and combining its contents into another macro.
